title: Compute diagnostics and part-worth exports from completed HB-Stan chain CSVs
summary: Load chain CSVs from a completed fit run and write three output files: diagnostics.json (per-parameter Rhat and ESS), summary.json (posterior mean, SD, and quantiles), and part_worths.csv (U × P matrix of posterior-mean part-worth utilities). Uses all post-warmup draws. Idempotent — safe to re-run.
description: Reads all hb_fit_chain_*.csv files from a completed `fit` run and computes three outputs using all post-warmup draws: convergence diagnostics (Rhat and ESS per parameter), a posterior summary (mean, SD, and quantiles), and a per-respondent part-worth matrix. The chain CSVs are unmodified — `finalize` is purely a read-and-summarise step.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Directory containing hb_fit_chain_*.csv files from a previous `fit` run. All files matching the pattern are loaded as independent chains.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

details:
`finalize` is the post-processing step that turns raw CmdStan chain output into actionable deliverables. It loads all chain CSV files, combines them, and uses all post-warmup draws to compute:

- **diagnostics.json** — per-parameter Rhat (potential scale reduction factor) and ESS (effective sample size). Rhat values above 1.01 indicate chains that have not mixed; ESS below ~400 indicates the posterior is imprecisely estimated.

- **summary.json** — posterior mean, standard deviation, and quantiles (5%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 95%) for each population-level parameter.

- **part_worths.csv** — U × P matrix (U respondents, P attributes) of posterior-mean part-worth utilities, one row per respondent.

CmdStan's `num_warmup` parameter controls how many adaptation draws are discarded before sampling begins — `finalize` uses all `num_samples` post-warmup draws. Because `finalize` does not modify the chain CSVs, it is safe to re-run.
value:
On success, run_dir receives:

- diagnostics.json — per-parameter Rhat and ESS.
- summary.json — posterior mean, SD, and quantiles for population-level parameters.
- part_worths.csv — U × P matrix of posterior-mean part-worth utilities.

In protocol mode the plugin emits event.run.started, then event.run.finished (or event.run.failed).
notes: Rhat > 1.01 for any parameter is a convergence warning — extend the run before trusting the part-worths.
All post-warmup draws are used; adaptation draws are controlled by num_warmup at sampling time.
Chain CSVs are never modified — re-running is safe.

see_also.description: Produces the chain CSVs that this command processes.
Runs fit then finalize in a single call.
Finalizes every fold produced by cv-fit.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

artifacts.title: Convergence diagnostics
artifacts.description: Per-parameter Rhat (potential scale reduction factor) and ESS (effective sample size). Review this first — Rhat values above 1.01 indicate chains that have not mixed, and the model should be re-run with more warmup iterations or draws.

artifacts.title: Posterior parameter summary
artifacts.description: Posterior mean, standard deviation, and quantiles (5%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 95%) for each population-level parameter — the overall mean part-worths and correlation structure across respondents.

artifacts.title: Individual part-worth utilities
artifacts.description: The key output file. One row per respondent, one column per attribute, showing each respondent's posterior-mean preference utility. Feed this to sim_db_build (model_type=hb_stan) to build a market simulator for competitive-share or take-rate projections.

fields.name: (attribute columns)
fields.description: Posterior-mean utility estimate per attribute per respondent. Column names match the coded attribute names from the estimation file.
